header.title: Understanding Macro-nutrients
header.subtitle: Learn the fundamentals of protein, carbs, and fats

muscle.title: Muscle Repair & Growth:
muscle.desc: Essential for building and maintaining lean muscle mass

satiety.title: Satiety:
satiety.desc: Keeps you feeling full longer, reducing overall calorie intake

metabolic.title: Metabolic Boost:
metabolic.desc: Highest thermic effect of all macros (20-30% of calories burned during digestion)

target.title: Daily Target:
target.desc: For optimal health and muscle maintenance, aim for <1>1.6-2.2g per kg</1> of body weight. Athletes and those building muscle may need up to 2.5g/kg.

energy.title: Primary Energy Source:
energy.desc: Your body's preferred fuel for brain and physical activity

performance.title: Performance:
performance.desc: Critical for high-intensity exercise and athletic performance

nutrients.title: Fiber & Nutrients:
nutrients.desc: Whole carbs provide essential vitamins, minerals, and digestive health

target.title: Daily Target:
target.desc: For active individuals, aim for <1>3-6g per kg</1> of body weight. Sedentary individuals may need less (2-3g/kg). Focus on complex, whole-food sources.

hormone.title: Hormone Production:
hormone.desc: Essential for testosterone, estrogen, and other vital hormones

vitamin.title: Vitamin Absorption:
vitamin.desc: Required to absorb fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K)

brain.title: Brain Health:
brain.desc: Your brain is 60% fat - omega-3s are crucial for cognitive function

target.title: Daily Target:
target.desc: Aim for <1>0.8-1.2g per kg</1> of body weight, or 20-35% of total calories. Prioritize unsaturated fats (olive oil, nuts, fish) over saturated fats.

warning.title: Balance Recommendation
warning.desc: For optimal nutrition: Aim for at least 15-25% protein (muscle & satiety), 20-35% healthy fats (hormones), and 40-60% carbs (energy). Adjust your ratios to meet these targets.
